text:
Once upon a time in a small town nestled between rolling hills and an impenetrable forest, a wise old tortoise named Theodore moved through the village every day on his leisurely pace. His small shell contained a treasure of knowledge passed down through generations—it was the town's library, except in Theodore’s version, a boundlessly vast repository of stories, facts, and wisdom.

Theodore’s daily routine was unremarkable, until one autumn morning when a soft fog settled over the town, obscuring the world beyond his slow trot. For the first time, he found himself halted by the thick, oppressive veil. Not content with simply observing this strange phenomenon, curiosity got the better of him. He prodded his nose tentatively into the mist, and as he inched forward, tiny dewdrops clung to his antenna, sparkling in the sunlight like tiny diamonds.

Theodore ventured a bit further, becoming gradually aware of the unusual sounds—birds quivering, the rustle of
